title: Entity self-identification and notification
obligationText: Essential and important entities shall identify themselves and notify the competent authority or the single point of contact of their name, address and up-to-date contact details, including email addresses, IP ranges and telephone numbers, as well as the relevant sector and subsector referred to in Annexes I or II, and the Member States where they provide services.

steps: Verify entity has submitted self-identification notification to competent authority
Confirm notification contains all required information fields
Check confirmation of receipt from competent authority

title: Updating registration information upon changes
obligationText: Essential and important entities shall notify any changes to the details submitted pursuant to Article 3(4) without undue delay and in any event within two weeks from the date of the change.

steps: Verify procedure exists for updating registration details
Check that past changes were notified within two weeks
Review change log for completeness

title: Management body approval of cybersecurity risk-management measures
obligationText: The management bodies of essential and important entities shall approve the cybersecurity risk-management measures taken by those entities in order to comply with Article 21.

steps: Review board or management body meeting minutes for formal approval of cybersecurity measures
Verify approval covers all Article 21 risk-management measures
Confirm approval is documented and dated

title: Management body oversight of cybersecurity measures implementation
obligationText: The management bodies of essential and important entities shall oversee the implementation of cybersecurity risk-management measures referred to in Article 21.

steps: Verify management body receives regular reports on cybersecurity measures implementation
Review evidence of management body oversight activities
Confirm feedback loop between management body and implementation team

title: Management body accountability for non-compliance
obligationText: The management bodies may be held liable for infringements by the entity of Article 21. The accountability of the management body for cybersecurity shall not affect the liability of the entity under national and Union law.

steps: Verify management body members have acknowledged their accountability
Review governance charter for liability provisions
Confirm legal advice has been obtained on management body liability

title: Management body cybersecurity training obligation
obligationText: Members of the management bodies of essential and important entities shall be required to follow training, and shall encourage essential and important entities to offer similar training to their employees on a regular basis, in order to gain sufficient knowledge and skills to enable them to identify risks and assess cybersecurity risk-management practices and their impact on the services provided by the entity.

steps: Verify training records exist for all management body members
Confirm training covers risk identification and cybersecurity risk-management assessment
Check training is conducted on a regular basis

title: Staff cybersecurity training access
obligationText: Essential and important entities shall offer cybersecurity training to their employees on a regular basis, enabling them to acquire sufficient knowledge and skills to identify risks and assess cybersecurity risk-management practices.

steps: Review employee cybersecurity training programme
Verify regular training schedule exists and is followed
Check training completion rates across the organisation

title: Management body risk identification competence
obligationText: Members of the management bodies shall gain sufficient knowledge and skills to enable them to identify risks and assess cybersecurity risk-management practices and their impact on the services provided by the entity.

steps: Interview management body members on their cybersecurity risk knowledge
Verify competency assessments have been conducted
Review evidence of knowledge evaluation or certification

title: Appropriate and proportionate technical measures
obligationText: Essential and important entities shall take appropriate and proportionate technical, operational and organisational measures to manage the risks posed to the security of network and information systems which those entities use for their operations or for the provision of their services, and to prevent or minimise the impact of incidents on recipients of their services and on other services.

steps: Review overall risk management framework for appropriateness and proportionality
Verify technical, operational and organisational measures are documented
Assess measures against the entity's risk profile and service impact

title: State-of-the-art consideration in measures
obligationText: The measures referred to in paragraph 1 shall be based on an all-hazards approach that aims to protect network and information systems and the physical environment of those systems from incidents, and shall take into account the state of the art and applicable European and international standards, the cost of implementation, and the relevant guidelines and recommendations.

steps: Verify measures reference state-of-the-art technologies and standards
Review cost-of-implementation considerations in measure selection
Check alignment with applicable European and international standards

title: Risk assessment policy
obligationText: Entities shall establish and maintain a documented risk assessment policy that defines the methodology, scope, frequency and criteria for conducting risk analyses of network and information systems.

steps: Verify existence of a documented risk assessment policy
Review methodology, scope, frequency and criteria defined in the policy
Confirm policy has been approved by management body

title: Information system security policy
obligationText: Entities shall establish and maintain a documented information system security policy setting out the overarching security objectives, principles and rules governing the protection of network and information systems.

steps: Verify existence of an IS security policy
Review completeness of security objectives and principles
Confirm policy is communicated to all relevant stakeholders

title: Risk treatment plan
obligationText: Entities shall develop and implement risk treatment plans that identify appropriate measures to mitigate, transfer, accept or avoid identified risks to the security of network and information systems.

steps: Review risk treatment plan for completeness
Verify each identified risk has an assigned treatment option
Check implementation status of risk treatment measures

title: Residual risk acceptance
obligationText: Entities shall formally document and obtain management approval for the acceptance of residual risks that remain after the application of risk treatment measures.

steps: Verify residual risks are documented and quantified
Confirm management body has formally accepted residual risks
Review residual risk levels against defined risk appetite

title: Policy review cycle
obligationText: Entities shall review and update their risk analysis and information system security policies at regular intervals and whenever significant changes occur in operations or the threat landscape.

steps: Verify a defined review cycle exists for security policies
Check policy version history for evidence of regular reviews
Confirm reviews are triggered by significant changes

title: Incident detection capabilities
obligationText: Entities shall establish and maintain capabilities for the timely detection of incidents affecting the security of network and information systems, including monitoring and logging mechanisms.

steps: Review monitoring and detection architecture
Verify logging mechanisms are in place for critical systems
Test detection capabilities against simulated incident scenarios

title: Incident classification and severity assessment
obligationText: Entities shall define and implement an incident classification scheme that enables consistent categorisation and severity assessment of security incidents.

steps: Review incident classification scheme for completeness
Verify severity levels are clearly defined with criteria
Check scheme alignment with NIS2 significant incident criteria

title: Incident response procedures
obligationText: Entities shall establish documented incident response procedures covering containment, eradication and recovery actions for security incidents.

steps: Review incident response procedures for completeness
Verify procedures cover containment, eradication and recovery phases
Check procedures are accessible to incident response team

title: Incident escalation procedures
obligationText: Entities shall define and maintain incident escalation procedures specifying roles, responsibilities and escalation paths for handling security incidents of varying severity levels.

steps: Review escalation matrix for completeness
Verify escalation paths exist for all severity levels
Confirm contact information is current and accessible

title: Incident communication procedures
obligationText: Entities shall establish procedures for internal and external communication during and after security incidents, including communication with CSIRTs, competent authorities, and affected parties.

steps: Review incident communication procedures
Verify templates exist for internal and external notifications
Confirm CSIRT and competent authority contact details are current

title: Lessons learned from incidents
obligationText: Entities shall conduct post-incident reviews and document lessons learned to improve incident handling procedures and prevent recurrence of similar incidents.

steps: Review post-incident review reports
Verify lessons learned are documented and shared
Check improvement actions are tracked to completion

title: Business continuity plan development
obligationText: Entities shall develop and maintain documented business continuity plans that ensure the continuity of essential services and operations during and after security incidents.

steps: Review business continuity plan for completeness
Verify plan is based on a business impact analysis
Confirm recovery time and recovery point objectives are defined

title: Crisis management procedures
obligationText: Entities shall establish crisis management procedures that define governance structures, decision-making processes and communication protocols for managing major security incidents or crises.

steps: Review crisis management procedures
Verify crisis team roles and decision-making authority are defined
Confirm communication protocols are documented

title: Backup management
obligationText: Entities shall implement and maintain a backup management policy and procedures ensuring regular, tested backups of critical data and system configurations needed for service restoration.

steps: Review backup policy and schedule
Verify backups are performed as scheduled
Test backup restoration for critical systems
Verify backup integrity and completeness

title: Disaster recovery planning
obligationText: Entities shall establish and maintain disaster recovery plans defining procedures and resources for restoring network and information systems to full operational capability following a disruptive incident.

steps: Review disaster recovery plan for completeness
Verify recovery procedures are defined for critical systems
Confirm necessary resources and infrastructure are identified

title: Business continuity and DR testing and exercises
obligationText: Entities shall conduct regular testing and exercises of business continuity and disaster recovery plans to validate their effectiveness and identify areas for improvement.

steps: Review BCP/DR test and exercise schedule
Verify tests have been conducted as planned
Review test results and remediation of identified gaps

title: Supplier risk assessment
obligationText: Entities shall conduct risk assessments of their direct suppliers and service providers, evaluating the security practices, vulnerabilities and overall quality of products and services in the supply chain.

steps: Review supplier risk assessment methodology
Verify risk assessments have been conducted for critical suppliers
Check that assessment results inform supplier management decisions

title: Contractual security requirements for suppliers
obligationText: Entities shall include appropriate security requirements in contracts with their direct suppliers and service providers, addressing cybersecurity risk-management measures, incident notification obligations and audit rights.

steps: Review standard contract templates for security clauses
Verify contracts include incident notification and audit provisions
Check that security requirements are proportionate to supplier risk level

title: Supplier monitoring and review
obligationText: Entities shall establish processes for ongoing monitoring and periodic review of the security posture of their direct suppliers and service providers throughout the contractual relationship.

steps: Review supplier monitoring procedures
Verify periodic reviews are conducted for critical suppliers
Check that monitoring results trigger corrective actions where needed

title: ICT product and service security assessment
obligationText: Entities shall assess the overall quality and resilience of ICT products and services, taking into account cybersecurity-related aspects and the cybersecurity practices of suppliers, including their secure development procedures.

steps: Review ICT product and service security assessment process
Verify assessments cover supplier secure development practices
Check that assessment outcomes inform procurement decisions

title: Secure development lifecycle
obligationText: Entities shall implement a secure development lifecycle for the acquisition, development and maintenance of network and information systems, incorporating security requirements from design through deployment.

steps: Review secure development lifecycle documentation
Verify security requirements are defined at each development phase
Check evidence of security reviews and code analysis

title: Vulnerability handling procedures
obligationText: Entities shall establish and apply procedures for identifying, evaluating, prioritising, treating and tracking vulnerabilities in their network and information systems.

steps: Review vulnerability management procedures
Verify regular vulnerability scanning is performed
Check remediation tracking and prioritisation criteria

title: Vulnerability disclosure policy
obligationText: Entities shall establish and implement a coordinated vulnerability disclosure policy enabling the responsible reporting and handling of discovered vulnerabilities in their products or services.

steps: Verify a vulnerability disclosure policy is published
Review the process for receiving and handling vulnerability reports
Check coordination procedures with CSIRTs and other parties

title: Patch management procedures
obligationText: Entities shall implement patch management procedures ensuring timely application of security patches and updates to network and information systems, prioritised based on risk assessment.

steps: Review patch management policy and procedures
Verify patch compliance rates for critical systems
Check that patching is prioritised based on vulnerability severity and risk

title: Security testing in acquisition and development
obligationText: Entities shall perform security testing of network and information systems during acquisition, development and maintenance to identify and address security weaknesses before deployment.

steps: Review security testing methodology and plan
Verify security tests are performed prior to deployment
Check that identified weaknesses are remediated before go-live

title: Cybersecurity risk-management effectiveness assessment policy
obligationText: Entities shall establish policies and procedures for regularly assessing the effectiveness of cybersecurity risk-management measures to ensure they continue to adequately address the security of network and information systems.

steps: Verify existence of effectiveness assessment policy
Review assessment methodology and schedule
Confirm assessments are conducted as planned

title: Security metrics and KPIs
obligationText: Entities shall define, collect and report on cybersecurity metrics and key performance indicators to measure the effectiveness of their cybersecurity risk-management measures.

steps: Review defined security metrics and KPIs
Verify metrics are collected and reported regularly
Check that metrics inform decision-making on security improvements

title: Vulnerability assessments
obligationText: Entities shall conduct regular vulnerability assessments of their network and information systems to identify security weaknesses and inform risk treatment decisions.

steps: Review vulnerability assessment schedule and scope
Verify assessments are conducted at defined intervals
Check remediation of identified vulnerabilities is tracked

title: Penetration testing
obligationText: Entities shall conduct penetration testing at appropriate intervals to evaluate the resilience of network and information systems against real-world attack scenarios.

steps: Review penetration testing schedule and scope
Verify penetration tests are conducted by qualified testers
Check that findings are remediated in a timely manner

title: Basic cyber hygiene practices
obligationText: Entities shall implement and maintain basic cyber hygiene practices including secure configuration, password management, software updates, network segmentation and endpoint protection.

steps: Review cyber hygiene practices documentation
Verify implementation of baseline security configurations
Check compliance with defined cyber hygiene standards

title: Security awareness training programme
obligationText: Entities shall establish and maintain a cybersecurity awareness training programme for all personnel, covering current threats, safe practices and the entity's security policies and procedures.

steps: Review security awareness training programme content
Verify training completion rates across the organisation
Check effectiveness through phishing simulation or knowledge assessments

title: Role-specific cybersecurity training
obligationText: Entities shall provide role-specific cybersecurity training to personnel with specialised security responsibilities, ensuring competence in their assigned security functions.

steps: Identify roles requiring specialised security training
Verify training plans exist for each specialised role
Check training completion and competency assessment records

title: Cryptographic policy
obligationText: Entities shall establish policies and procedures regarding the use of cryptography, defining approved algorithms, key lengths and use cases for cryptographic controls.

steps: Review cryptographic policy for completeness
Verify approved algorithms and key lengths are defined
Check policy covers all relevant use cases

title: Encryption deployment for data protection
obligationText: Entities shall deploy encryption where appropriate to protect the confidentiality and integrity of data at rest and in transit across their network and information systems.

steps: Verify encryption is deployed for data at rest and in transit
Check encryption implementation against cryptographic policy
Test that encryption mechanisms are functioning correctly

title: Cryptographic key management
obligationText: Entities shall establish and maintain key management procedures covering key generation, distribution, storage, rotation, revocation and destruction throughout the cryptographic key lifecycle.

steps: Review key management procedures
Verify key lifecycle management covers all phases
Check key storage and access controls are appropriate

title: Human resources security policy
obligationText: Entities shall establish human resources security policies addressing security responsibilities of personnel before, during and after employment, including onboarding, role changes and termination procedures.

steps: Review HR security policy for completeness
Verify onboarding and offboarding procedures include security provisions
Check policy covers role changes and employment termination

title: Personnel background checks
obligationText: Entities shall conduct appropriate background verification checks on personnel prior to granting access to sensitive network and information systems, in accordance with applicable laws.

steps: Review background verification policy and criteria
Verify checks are conducted for personnel with access to sensitive systems
Confirm compliance with applicable legal requirements

title: Access control policy
obligationText: Entities shall establish and implement access control policies based on the principles of least privilege and need-to-know, governing logical and physical access to network and information systems.

steps: Review access control policy for least privilege and need-to-know principles
Verify access matrix covers critical systems
Check that access rights are assigned based on defined roles

title: Privileged access management
obligationText: Entities shall implement privileged access management controls including enhanced authentication, monitoring and logging of all privileged access to network and information systems.

steps: Review privileged access management controls
Verify privileged account inventory is complete and current
Test monitoring and logging of privileged access activities

title: Identity management and access reviews
obligationText: Entities shall implement identity management processes including unique user identification, authentication mechanisms and periodic access reviews to ensure access rights remain appropriate.

steps: Review identity management processes
Verify periodic access reviews are conducted
Check that stale or excessive access rights are revoked promptly

title: Asset management and inventory
obligationText: Entities shall maintain an up-to-date inventory of assets including hardware, software, data and network components relevant to the security of their network and information systems.

steps: Review asset inventory for completeness
Verify inventory includes all critical system components
Check that inventory is regularly updated

title: Multi-factor authentication deployment
obligationText: Entities shall deploy multi-factor authentication solutions for access to network and information systems, in particular for remote access and access to critical or sensitive systems.

steps: Verify MFA is deployed for remote access and critical systems
Test MFA mechanisms are functioning correctly
Review coverage of MFA across the entity's systems

title: Continuous authentication solutions
obligationText: Entities shall implement, where appropriate, continuous authentication solutions to monitor and validate user identity and access throughout active sessions on network and information systems.

steps: Review continuous authentication implementation
Verify session monitoring and validation mechanisms
Test session timeout and re-authentication controls

title: Secured voice, video and text communications
obligationText: Entities shall ensure secured voice, video and text communications within the entity, using encryption and appropriate security controls to protect the confidentiality and integrity of communications.

steps: Review secure communications policy and standards
Verify encryption is applied to voice, video and text communications
Test security controls on communication platforms

title: Secured emergency communication systems
obligationText: Entities shall establish and maintain secured emergency communication systems enabling reliable and protected communications during incidents, crises and service disruptions.

steps: Review emergency communication system design
Verify system availability and resilience
Test emergency communication channels during exercises

title: All-hazards approach to risk management
obligationText: When assessing the proportionality of measures, entities shall take due account of the degree of exposure to risks, the size of the entity, the likelihood of occurrence of incidents and their severity, including their societal and economic impact.

steps: Verify risk assessments consider all types of hazards
Review proportionality analysis for measures taken
Confirm entity size, exposure and impact factors are considered

title: Compliance with European and international standards
obligationText: Member States shall ensure that, where relevant, entities use European and international standards and technical specifications relevant to the security of network and information systems when implementing cybersecurity risk-management measures.

steps: Review alignment with relevant European and international standards
Verify standards mapping to implemented measures
Check for applicable certifications

title: Compliance with delegated and implementing acts
obligationText: Entities shall comply with any delegated or implementing acts adopted by the Commission that lay down the technical and methodological requirements of the cybersecurity risk-management measures referred to in Article 21.

steps: Identify applicable delegated and implementing acts
Review compliance status against each applicable act
Verify gap analysis and remediation plans are in place

title: Significant incident notification obligation
obligationText: Essential and important entities shall notify, without undue delay, the CSIRT or, where applicable, the competent authority of any significant incident that has a significant impact on the provision of their services.

steps: Verify incident notification procedure is documented
Confirm CSIRT and competent authority contact information is current
Review notification templates for completeness

title: Forwarding notification to CSIRT or competent authority
obligationText: Where the entity notifies the competent authority of a significant incident, the Member State shall ensure that the competent authority forwards the notification to the CSIRT upon receipt. Where the entity notifies the CSIRT, the CSIRT shall forward the notification to the competent authority.

steps: Verify entity procedure accounts for forwarding requirements
Confirm dual notification path is documented
Review past notification forwarding records

title: Early warning submission within 24 hours
obligationText: An early warning shall be submitted without undue delay and in any event within 24 hours of the entity becoming aware of the significant incident, indicating whether the significant incident is suspected of being caused by unlawful or malicious acts.

steps: Verify early warning process can meet 24-hour requirement
Review early warning template for required content
Check past incident timelines for compliance

title: Early warning content requirements
obligationText: The early warning shall indicate whether the significant incident is suspected of being caused by unlawful or malicious acts and whether it could have a cross-border impact.

steps: Verify early warning template includes malicious act indicator field
Confirm cross-border impact assessment is included
Review completeness of early warning content requirements

title: Suspected malicious act indicator in early warning
obligationText: Where the entity suspects the significant incident is caused by unlawful or malicious acts, the early warning shall include an indication of the suspected nature and any initial indicators of compromise available.

steps: Verify procedure for documenting suspected malicious acts
Check that IoC reporting fields are included in early warning template
Review past early warnings for compliance with this requirement

title: Incident notification within 72 hours
obligationText: An incident notification shall be submitted without undue delay and in any event within 72 hours of the entity becoming aware of the significant incident, updating the information provided in the early warning.

steps: Verify notification process can meet 72-hour requirement
Review notification template for completeness
Check past incident timelines for compliance

title: Initial assessment in incident notification
obligationText: The incident notification shall include an initial assessment of the significant incident, including its severity and impact, as well as, where available, the indicators of compromise.

steps: Verify notification template includes initial assessment fields
Check severity and impact assessment criteria are defined
Review IoC reporting mechanism in the notification

title: Severity and impact assessment in notification
obligationText: The incident notification shall provide information on the severity and impact of the incident on the services provided by the entity, including the number of recipients affected.

steps: Verify notification template captures service impact information
Check methodology for determining affected recipients
Review severity classification criteria

title: Intermediate report upon request
obligationText: Upon request of the CSIRT or competent authority, entities shall submit an intermediate report with relevant status updates on the significant incident and its handling.

steps: Verify procedure exists for responding to intermediate report requests
Review intermediate report template for completeness
Check that status update process is documented

title: Final report submission within one month
obligationText: A final report shall be submitted not later than one month after the submission of the incident notification under paragraph 4(b), unless the incident is still ongoing at that time.

steps: Verify final report process and one-month deadline tracking
Review final report template for required sections
Check past incidents for timely final report submission

title: Detailed incident description in final report
obligationText: The final report shall include a detailed description of the incident, including its severity and impact.

steps: Verify final report template requires detailed incident description
Check that severity and impact sections are comprehensive
Review past final reports for descriptive quality

title: Threat type and root cause in final report
obligationText: The final report shall include the type of threat or root cause that is likely to have triggered the incident.

steps: Verify final report template includes threat type and root cause fields
Review root cause analysis methodology
Check past reports for adequate threat classification

title: Applied and ongoing mitigation measures in final report
obligationText: The final report shall include the applied and ongoing mitigation measures taken to address the incident and reduce its impact.

steps: Verify final report template includes mitigation measures section
Check that both applied and ongoing measures are documented
Review remediation tracking for completeness

title: Cross-border impact in final report
obligationText: Where applicable, the final report shall include the cross-border impact of the significant incident on other Member States.

steps: Verify final report template includes cross-border impact assessment
Review methodology for assessing cross-border impact
Check that relevant Member States are identified

title: Progress report for ongoing incidents
obligationText: In the event that the incident is still ongoing at the time of the submission of the final report, the entity shall provide a progress report at that time and a final report within one month of its handling of the incident.

steps: Verify procedure for progress reports on ongoing incidents
Review progress report template for required content
Check that final report deadline is tracked from incident resolution

title: Information to recipients and users about significant threats
obligationText: Entities shall, where appropriate, inform the recipients of their services without undue delay of significant threats that are likely to adversely affect the provision of those services, and of any measures or remedies those recipients can take in response to that threat.

steps: Verify procedure for informing service recipients of significant threats
Review notification template for recommended remedial measures
Check timeliness of past recipient notifications

title: Information sharing with affected Member States
obligationText: Where a significant incident affects or is likely to affect another Member State, the CSIRT, competent authority or single point of contact shall, without undue delay, inform the affected Member State's single points of contact.

steps: Verify cross-border notification procedure is documented
Check that contact points for other Member States are maintained
Review entity's role in facilitating cross-border information sharing

title: Public disclosure of significant incidents
obligationText: Where public awareness is necessary to prevent a significant incident or to deal with an ongoing significant incident, or where disclosure of the significant incident is otherwise in the public interest, the CSIRT or competent authority may inform the public, or may require the entity to do so.

steps: Verify public disclosure policy is documented
Review media communication templates and protocols
Check procedure for complying with authority-mandated disclosures

title: Reporting templates and procedures compliance
obligationText: Entities shall use the reporting templates and procedures established by the CSIRT or competent authority for submitting incident notifications, early warnings and final reports.

steps: Verify entity uses prescribed reporting templates
Check alignment with authority-defined procedures
Review past submissions for template compliance

title: Use of EU cybersecurity certification schemes
obligationText: In order to demonstrate compliance with certain requirements of Article 21, Member States may require essential and important entities to use particular ICT products, ICT services and ICT processes, which are certified under European cybersecurity certification schemes adopted pursuant to Regulation (EU) 2019/881.

steps: Identify applicable EU cybersecurity certification requirements
Review ICT product and service inventory against certification schemes
Verify certified products are used where required

title: Commission implementing acts on certification requirements
obligationText: The Commission is empowered to adopt implementing acts specifying which categories of essential or important entities shall be required to use certain certified ICT products, services or processes, or obtain a certificate under a European cybersecurity certification scheme.

steps: Monitor for applicable Commission implementing acts on certification
Verify compliance plan exists for anticipated certification requirements
Track implementation status of any mandatory certification

title: Use of European and international standards
obligationText: Without imposing or discriminating in favour of the use of a particular type of technology, Member States shall encourage the use of European and international standards and technical specifications relevant to the security of network and information systems.

steps: Review use of European and international standards in security measures
Verify standards mapping to implemented controls
Check for technology-neutral approach in standards adoption

title: Commission recommendations on standardisation
obligationText: ENISA, in cooperation with Member States, shall draw up advice and guidelines regarding the technical areas to be considered in relation to standardisation, as well as existing standards and technical specifications, including national standards.

steps: Review ENISA guidelines on applicable technical standards
Verify entity considers ENISA recommendations in standards adoption
Track compliance with emerging standardisation requirements

title: DNS registries and registrars data obligations
obligationText: TLD name registries and the entities providing domain name registration services shall collect and maintain accurate and complete domain name registration data in a dedicated database with due diligence in accordance with Union data protection law.

steps: Verify domain registration data collection procedures
Review database for accuracy and completeness
Check compliance with Union data protection law

title: WHOIS database maintenance
obligationText: TLD name registries and the entities providing domain name registration services shall make publicly available the domain name registration data that is not personal data, through a WHOIS query service.

steps: Verify WHOIS query service is operational
Check that non-personal registration data is publicly accessible
Test WHOIS service response accuracy

title: Legitimate access to domain registration data
obligationText: TLD name registries and the entities providing domain name registration services shall have policies and procedures in place to ensure that their databases include accurate and complete information, and shall grant access to specific domain name registration data upon lawful and duly justified requests by legitimate access seekers.

steps: Review policies for granting legitimate access to registration data
Verify request handling procedures are documented
Check access logs for compliance with access policy

title: Domain registration data accuracy policy
obligationText: TLD name registries and the entities providing domain name registration services shall put in place policies and procedures including verification procedures to ensure that the databases contain accurate and complete information.

steps: Review data accuracy policies and verification procedures
Verify data quality assurance measures are implemented
Check recent verification activity records

title: Domain name registration data collection and maintenance
obligationText: TLD name registries and the entities providing domain name registration services shall collect and maintain accurate and complete domain name registration data including the name, email address and telephone number of the domain name registrant and the contact point administering the domain name.

steps: Verify data collection includes all required registration data fields
Review database schema for data completeness
Check data maintenance procedures are followed

title: Verification of domain registration data
obligationText: TLD name registries and the entities providing domain name registration services shall put in place policies and procedures including verification procedures to ensure that the databases referred to in paragraph 1 include accurate and complete information.

steps: Review verification policies and procedures
Verify that verification is performed on registration data
Check verification records for recent activity

title: Legitimate access request handling for registration data
obligationText: TLD name registries and the entities providing domain name registration services shall reply without undue delay and in any event within 72 hours of the receipt of any requests for access to domain name registration data from legitimate access seekers.

steps: Verify access request handling procedure meets 72-hour response requirement
Review request log for response timeliness
Check legitimate access seeker verification process

title: Voluntary notification of cyber threats
obligationText: Entities may notify, on a voluntary basis, to the CSIRT or, where applicable, to the competent authority, incidents, cyber threats and near misses.

steps: Verify procedure for voluntary notification of cyber threats exists
Review notification templates for threat and near-miss reporting
Check records of past voluntary notifications

title: Cybersecurity information sharing arrangements
obligationText: Essential and important entities may exchange relevant cybersecurity information amongst themselves, including information relating to cyber threats, vulnerabilities, indicators of compromise, tactics, techniques and procedures, cybersecurity alerts and configuration tools, on a voluntary basis.

steps: Review information sharing arrangements and agreements
Verify participation in relevant sharing communities or platforms
Check data handling provisions within sharing agreements

title: Notification to competent authority about sharing arrangements
obligationText: Essential and important entities shall notify the competent authority of their participation in the cybersecurity information sharing arrangements referred to in paragraph 2, upon entering into such arrangements, or, where applicable, their withdrawal from such arrangements.

steps: Verify notification to competent authority upon entering sharing arrangements
Check procedure for notifying withdrawal from arrangements
Review confirmation records from competent authority

title: Cooperation with on-site inspections
obligationText: Essential entities shall cooperate with and facilitate on-site inspections carried out by competent authorities, including providing access to premises, systems and relevant documentation.

steps: Verify on-site inspection readiness procedures exist
Review past inspection records for cooperation
Confirm access arrangements are documented

title: Cooperation with regular and targeted security audits
obligationText: Essential entities shall facilitate and cooperate with regular audits and targeted security audits carried out by an independent body or a competent authority.

steps: Verify audit readiness procedures are documented
Review past audit reports and entity cooperation records
Confirm audit finding remediation is tracked

title: Cooperation with ad hoc audits
obligationText: Essential entities shall cooperate with ad hoc audits justified by a significant incident or an infringement of this Directive by the entity.

steps: Verify procedure for ad hoc audit cooperation exists
Review readiness to support unscheduled audits
Confirm resources are available for ad hoc audit support

title: Cooperation with security scans
obligationText: Essential entities shall cooperate with security scans carried out by the competent authority or by a qualified provider appointed by the competent authority, based on objective, non-discriminatory, fair and transparent risk assessment criteria.

steps: Verify procedure for cooperating with authority security scans
Review past security scan results and entity response
Confirm remediation of scan findings is tracked

title: Response to information requests from authorities
obligationText: Essential entities shall provide the competent authority with the information requested, including documented evidence of implementation of cybersecurity policies, necessary for supervision.

steps: Verify procedure for responding to authority information requests
Review evidence repository for readiness
Check past responses for completeness and timeliness

title: Data access for supervisory purposes
obligationText: Essential entities shall provide access to data, documents and information needed by the competent authority for the performance of its supervisory tasks, including data necessary to verify compliance with this Directive.

steps: Verify procedure for providing data access to supervisory authorities
Review data inventory for supervisory-relevant information
Confirm access can be provided in a timely manner

title: Evidence of cybersecurity policy implementation
obligationText: Essential entities shall provide documented evidence of the effective implementation of cybersecurity policies, including the results of security audits carried out by a qualified auditor and the respective underlying evidence.

steps: Verify documented evidence of cybersecurity policy implementation
Review security audit reports from qualified auditors
Check that underlying evidence supports audit findings

title: Readiness for compliance order issuance
obligationText: Essential entities shall be prepared for competent authorities to issue binding instructions or orders requiring the entity to remedy deficiencies identified or prevent infringements of this Directive.

steps: Verify procedure exists for handling compliance orders
Review remediation framework capabilities
Confirm resources are allocated for enforcement response

title: Cooperation with ex-post on-site inspections
obligationText: Important entities shall cooperate with on-site inspections carried out ex post by competent authorities following evidence, indication or information that the entity is allegedly not complying with this Directive.

steps: Verify procedure for cooperating with ex-post inspections
Review past inspection cooperation records
Confirm access arrangements for authority inspectors

title: Cooperation with ex-post targeted security audits
obligationText: Important entities shall cooperate with targeted security audits carried out by an independent body or a competent authority based on evidence, indication or information of alleged non-compliance.

steps: Verify targeted security audit cooperation procedures
Review audit readiness documentation
Confirm audit finding remediation is tracked

title: Cooperation with ex-post security scans
obligationText: Important entities shall cooperate with security scans carried out by competent authorities or qualified providers based on objective, non-discriminatory, fair and transparent risk assessment criteria.

steps: Verify procedure for cooperating with security scans
Review past scan results and remediation actions
Confirm cooperation with qualified scan providers

title: Response to ex-post information requests
obligationText: Important entities shall provide the competent authority with information necessary to assess the cybersecurity risk-management measures adopted by the entity, including documented cybersecurity policies, and shall comply with the obligation to submit information or evidence of implementation of those policies.

steps: Verify information request response procedure exists
Review evidence package readiness
Confirm documented policies are available for submission

title: Readiness for enforcement measures (important entities)
obligationText: Important entities shall be prepared for competent authorities to issue binding instructions or orders requiring the entity to take measures necessary to prevent or remedy an incident, including the measures and deadlines for implementation.

steps: Verify enforcement readiness procedures exist
Review remediation framework for enforcement response capability
Confirm resources and processes for implementing binding instructions

title: Readiness for public notification of non-compliance
obligationText: Important entities shall be prepared for competent authorities to publicly notify aspects of non-compliance with this Directive and to make public the identity of the entity and the nature of the non-compliance.

steps: Verify entity has a response plan for public non-compliance notifications
Review communications procedures for reputational management
Confirm stakeholder communication plans are in place

title: Readiness for notification warnings to entity clients
obligationText: Essential entities shall be prepared for competent authorities to require the entity to inform natural or legal persons with regard to which it provides services or carries out activities which are potentially affected by a significant cyber threat, of the nature of the threat and of any protective measures.

steps: Verify procedure for notifying clients of significant cyber threats
Review notification templates for completeness
Confirm stakeholder contact lists are maintained

title: Readiness for implementing audit recommendations
obligationText: Essential entities shall be prepared for competent authorities to require the entity to implement the recommendations provided as a result of a security audit within a reasonable deadline.

steps: Verify process for tracking and implementing audit recommendations
Review remediation timelines and progress
Confirm adequate resources are allocated for implementation

title: Readiness for public disclosure of non-compliance (essential entities)
obligationText: Essential entities shall be prepared for competent authorities to make publicly available a statement identifying the entity and the nature and details of the non-compliance, including remedial measures taken.

steps: Verify response plan for public disclosure of non-compliance
Review communications strategy for reputational management
Confirm legal review process is in place

title: Readiness for administrative fines
obligationText: Essential entities shall be aware that competent authorities may impose administrative fines in accordance with Article 34 for infringements of Articles 21 or 23, and shall maintain governance structures to prevent and address non-compliance.

steps: Verify awareness of administrative fine provisions
Review compliance governance structures
Confirm risk register includes non-compliance financial exposure

title: Readiness for temporary management prohibition
obligationText: Essential entities shall be aware that competent authorities may request that relevant bodies or courts temporarily prohibit any person responsible for discharging managerial responsibilities from exercising managerial functions in that entity.

steps: Verify management body is aware of temporary prohibition provisions
Review succession planning for key management positions
Confirm accountability framework addresses enforcement scenarios

title: Readiness for implementing audit recommendations (important entities)
obligationText: Important entities shall be prepared for competent authorities to require the entity to implement the recommendations provided as a result of a security audit within a reasonable deadline.

steps: Verify process for tracking audit recommendation implementation
Review remediation timelines and progress tracking
Confirm resource allocation for audit remediation

title: Readiness for administrative fines (important entities)
obligationText: Important entities shall be aware that competent authorities may impose administrative fines in accordance with Article 34 for infringements of Articles 21 or 23, and shall maintain appropriate governance to prevent non-compliance.

steps: Verify awareness of administrative fine provisions
Review compliance governance framework
Confirm financial exposure from non-compliance is assessed

title: Roles and responsibilities for information security
obligationText: Entities shall define and document roles and responsibilities for the security of network and information systems, including assignment of security functions and clear reporting lines.

steps: Review documented roles and responsibilities for IS security
Verify clear reporting lines are established
Confirm all critical security functions have assigned owners

title: Incident response team establishment
obligationText: Entities shall establish and maintain a dedicated or designated incident response team with defined roles, competencies, tools and authority to manage security incidents effectively.

steps: Review incident response team charter and composition
Verify team members have required competencies
Confirm team has necessary tools and authority

title: Business impact analysis
obligationText: Entities shall conduct and maintain a business impact analysis identifying critical business processes, dependencies, and the potential impact of their disruption on service delivery.

steps: Review business impact analysis for completeness
Verify critical processes and dependencies are identified
Confirm impact levels and recovery priorities are defined

title: Supply chain incident notification
obligationText: Entities shall establish requirements for suppliers and service providers to notify the entity of security incidents that may affect the security of the entity's network and information systems.

steps: Verify supplier contracts include incident notification requirements
Review notification procedures between entity and suppliers
Check past supplier incident notification records

title: Network segmentation and architecture security
obligationText: Entities shall implement appropriate network segmentation and security architecture controls to limit the propagation of incidents and protect critical network and information system components.

steps: Review network architecture and segmentation design
Verify segmentation controls are implemented and effective
Test network segregation between critical and non-critical zones

title: Change management for network and information systems
obligationText: Entities shall establish change management procedures ensuring that changes to network and information systems are assessed for security impact, approved, documented and tested before implementation.

steps: Review change management procedures
Verify changes are assessed for security impact before approval
Check change request records for documentation completeness

title: Internal security audits
obligationText: Entities shall conduct internal security audits at planned intervals to evaluate the conformity and effectiveness of cybersecurity risk-management measures and identify areas for improvement.

steps: Review internal security audit programme and schedule
Verify audits are conducted at planned intervals
Check audit findings remediation tracking and closure

title: Physical access control
obligationText: Entities shall implement physical access controls to protect the physical environment of network and information systems, including access restrictions, visitor management and environmental security measures.

steps: Review physical access control policy
Inspect physical access control mechanisms at key facilities
Verify visitor management procedures are followed

title: Management body personal liability acknowledgement
obligationText: Members of management bodies of essential and important entities shall acknowledge that they can be held liable in accordance with national law for infringements by the entity of the obligations under Article 21, ensuring personal accountability for cybersecurity governance.

steps: Verify management body members have signed liability acknowledgements
Review governance charter for personal accountability provisions
Confirm legal briefing has been provided on liability obligations

title: Phishing and social engineering awareness
obligationText: Entities shall include phishing simulation exercises and social engineering awareness as part of their basic cyber hygiene practices to build resilience against social engineering attacks.

steps: Review phishing simulation programme and frequency
Verify simulation results are tracked and reported
Check that results inform targeted training for vulnerable users

title: Indicators of compromise in incident notification
obligationText: The incident notification shall include, where available, the indicators of compromise relevant to the significant incident to enable the CSIRT and competent authority to assess the threat.

steps: Verify procedure for collecting and reporting indicators of compromise
Review IoC reporting template for completeness
Check that IoC collection is integrated into incident response workflow

title: Proportionality of measures to entity risk exposure
obligationText: The measures referred to in paragraph 1 shall be proportionate to the risk exposure of the entity, taking into account the entity's size, the likelihood and severity of incidents, and the societal and economic impact.

steps: Review proportionality assessment of cybersecurity measures
Verify entity size and risk exposure factors are considered
Confirm measures are justified relative to the entity's risk profile
